mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and real estate investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60d returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index below its 6-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side contraction, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to positive during post-shock recovery periods, or Rank IC remains negative across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation months consistently produce positive 5d returns, or signal fails to predict direction in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment and higher aggregate income, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20d forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 0.5 percent signals severe economic deceleration and contracting corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Low GDP growth quarters yield positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances, or signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and rising unemployment confirm a broad-based economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined negative signals fail to predict negative returns, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant in walk-forward validation.
